title: ConfigurationFeatureAspectColumn Object
description: Represents a feature aspect column in a configuration table. Feature aspects are properties of a feature that are unique to a particular type of feature. This includes various thread and joint settings.

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

methods.name: deleteMe
methods.description: Deletes this column from the table. Property columns cannot be deleted. If the table was obtained from a DataFile, this method will fail.

methods.name: getCell
methods.description: Gets the cell in this column at the specified row. Depending on the type of data in the cells within the column a ConfigurationFeatureAspectBooleanCell or ConfigurationFeatureAspectStringCell will be returned. The first row has an index of 0 and does not include the header row.

methods.name: getCellByRowId
methods.description: This method returns the cell in this column at the row identified by its ID. Depending on the type of data in the cells within the column, a ConfigurationFeatureAspectBooleanCell or ConfigurationFeatureAspectStringCell will be returned.

methods.name: getCellByRowName
methods.description: Gets the cell in this column at the row specified by its name. Depending on the type of data in the cells within the column a ConfigurationFeatureAspectBooleanCell or ConfigurationFeatureAspectStringCell will be returned.

properties.name: aspectType
properties.description: Gets the type of feature aspect this column is controlling.

properties.name: feature
properties.description: Returns the feature being controlled by this column.   This property returns null when the table being queried was obtained from a DataFile object.

properties.name: id
properties.description: The id of the column. This is constant and cannot be changed by the user.

properties.name: index
properties.description: The index position of this column within the table. The first column is at index 0 and does not include the "Name" column.

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

properties.name: parentTable
properties.description: This property returns the parent table, either the top or custom theme table this column is in.

properties.name: rowCount
properties.description: Returns the number of rows in this column.

properties.name: title
properties.description: The title of this column. In a top table, this can only be edited for suppression, visibility, parameter, and theme table columns. It behaves as read-only for all other types. In a theme table, the title of all the columns can be modified except for the column that represents the root component for materials and appearances.   If the table was obtained from a DataFile, this property behaves as read-only for all the columns.

aspectType.title: ConfigurationFeatureAspectColumn.aspectType Property
aspectType.description: Gets the type of feature aspect this column is controlling.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = configurationFeatureAspectColumn_var.aspectType

title.title: ConfigurationFeatureAspectColumn.title Property
title.description: The title of this column. In a top table, this can only be edited for suppression, visibility, parameter, and theme table columns. It behaves as read-only for all other types. In a theme table, the title of all the columns can be modified except for the column that represents the root component for materials and appearances. If the table was obtained from a DataFile, this property behaves as read-only for all the columns.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = configurationFeatureAspectColumn_var.title

# Set the value of the property.
configurationFeatureAspectColumn_var.title = propertyValue

classType.title: ConfigurationFeatureAspectColumn.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().
classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.

deleteMe.title: ConfigurationFeatureAspectColumn.deleteMe Method
deleteMe.description: Deletes this column from the table. Property columns cannot be deleted. If the table was obtained from a DataFile, this method will fail.
deleteMe.return_description: Returns true if the deletion was successful.

getCell.title: ConfigurationFeatureAspectColumn.getCell Method
getCell.description: Gets the cell in this column at the specified row. Depending on the type of data in the cells within the column a ConfigurationFeatureAspectBooleanCell or ConfigurationFeatureAspectStringCell will be returned. The first row has an index of 0 and does not include the header row.
getCell.return_description: Returns the specified cell if successful and null if an invalid index was specified.

parameters.name: rowIndex
parameters.description: The index of the row to return the cell for. The first row has an index of 0.

getCellByRowId.title: ConfigurationFeatureAspectColumn.getCellByRowId Method
getCellByRowId.description: This method returns the cell in this column at the row identified by its ID. Depending on the type of data in the cells within the column, a ConfigurationFeatureAspectBooleanCell or ConfigurationFeatureAspectStringCell will be returned.
getCellByRowId.return_description: Returns the specified cell if successful and null if the id is not found.

parameters.name: rowId
parameters.description: The ID of the row to return the cell for.

getCellByRowName.title: ConfigurationFeatureAspectColumn.getCellByRowName Method
getCellByRowName.description: Gets the cell in this column at the row specified by its name. Depending on the type of data in the cells within the column a ConfigurationFeatureAspectBooleanCell or ConfigurationFeatureAspectStringCell will be returned.
getCellByRowName.return_description: Returns the specified cell if successful and null if the name is not found.

parameters.name: rowName
parameters.description: The name of the row to return the cell for.
